description: New York City borough in which the building is located.
Neighborhood associated with the building.
Building Identification Number; unique building-level identifier.
Roof height of the building.
Original number of floors from the source dataset.

Land-use code from a first merge/source table. Code mapping: 01 = One & Two Family Buildings; 02 = Multi-Family Walk-Up Buildings; 03 = Multi-Family Elevator Buildings; 04 = Mixed Residential & Commercial Buildings; 05 = Commercial & Office Buildings; 06 = Industrial & Manufacturing; 07 = Transportation & Utility; 08 = Public Facilities & Institutions; 09 = Open Space & Outdoor Recreation; 10 = Parking Facilities; 11 = Vacant Land.
Building class / building typology code from NYC DOF / PLUTO. See the 'Code Mappings' sheet for detailed code meanings (e.g., A4, C6, O5, D4).
Longitude of the building centroid.
Latitude of the building centroid.
Longitude of the address point.
Latitude of the address point.
Distance from the building-related point to the nearest sidewalk, in meters.
Normalized number of floors; proxy for building verticality.
Normalized road-to-delivery distance; proxy for curb-to-dropoff separation.

Normalized proxy for building shape complexity / elongation. Computed from building geometry, typically using a shape ratio / elongation measure and normalized to [0,1], where higher values indicate more elongated or complex footprints.
Normalized access-difficulty penalty derived from building land use. Mapping used: land use 4 or 5 -> 0.00; land use 1, 2, or 3 -> 0.33; land use 6 or 7 -> 0.66; other / fallback classes -> 1.00.
Normalized proxy for neighborhood population intensity.

Normalized pedestrian corridor / pedestrian presence indicator. Mapping used: baseline street -> 0.00; community connector -> 0.25; neighborhood corridor -> 0.50; regional corridor -> 0.75; global corridor -> 1.00.
Proxy for surrounding urban activity derived from land use. Mapping used: land use 1 -> 0.30; 2 -> 0.45; 3 -> 0.50; 4 -> 0.90; 5 -> 1.00; 6 -> 0.80; 7 -> 0.70; 8 -> 0.75; 9 -> 0.20; 10 or 11 -> 0.10.

Penalty associated with low pedestrian presence. Computed as 1 - pedestrian_corridor_presence_norm_R. Example mapping: baseline street -> 1.00; community connector -> 0.75; neighborhood corridor -> 0.50; regional corridor -> 0.25; global corridor -> 0.00.
Penalty indicating missing or weak sidewalk accessibility. Mapping: sidewalk reachable / present = 0; sidewalk absent or not reachable = 1.
Count of active parking meters in the building's curb context.

Penalty / scarcity proxy derived from parking meter availability. Built through inverse normalization: fewer active meters correspond to higher scarcity penalty, while more active meters correspond to lower scarcity penalty.
Count of curb regulation signs in the surrounding context.
Normalized intensity of curb restrictions. Higher values indicate a stronger concentration of curb regulation signs.
Vehicle-type or blockface context attribute from the curb dataset.
Flag indicating commercial or truck-oriented curb context. Mapping: commercial / truck-related context = 1; otherwise = 0.
Number of parking lanes associated with the nearby street segment.
Sum of the raw curb-difficulty components.

Final curb/parking difficulty proxy used in the car regression. Computed as the average of three components: parking-meter scarcity penalty, curb-restriction intensity, and commercial/truck curb-context flag; higher values indicate more difficult curbside conditions.
Predicted car last-meter delivery time in seconds.
Predicted AMR last-meter delivery time in seconds.
